scenarios.name: Interest Rate Shock
scenarios.description: Central bank announces an unexpected rate hike, impacting lending portfolios, mortgage books, and fixed income holdings. Financial institutions must assess exposure and adjust pricing strategies.

customization.context: Leading bank through sudden rate environment shift
Assessing portfolio duration and interest rate risk exposure
Managing funding costs and liquidity position
Forecasting rate trajectory and economic impact
Managing deposit pricing and mortgage portfolio
Evaluating bank equity and debt positions post-rate shock

round_structure: Rate announcement impact assessment: Portfolio exposure analysis
Funding cost recalculation: Deposit and wholesale funding impacts
Asset repricing strategy: Loan book adjustments
Hedging position review: Derivatives and duration management
Competitive response: Peer bank pricing moves
Capital adequacy check: Regulatory capital implications
Strategic pivot decision: Portfolio rebalancing commitments
Communication plan: Investor and customer messaging

required: Current interest rate exposure and duration gap
Loan book composition and repricing schedules
Deposit base maturity and beta analysis
Hedging portfolio positions and effectiveness

environment_config.decision_mechanism: Executive committee with risk committee oversight
environment_config.escalation_trigger: Capital ratio breach triggers emergency response
environment_config.coalition_dynamics: Treasury efficiency vs. customer retention priorities

scenarios.name: Credit Crisis Contagion
scenarios.description: A major financial institution faces failure, triggering systemic risk concerns. Banks must assess counterparty exposure, liquidity needs, and potential regulatory interventions.

customization.context: Navigating systemic crisis with institution at risk
Assessing counterparty and contagion risks
Managing liquidity and funding under stress
Advising on regulatory communications and legal exposure
Coordinating systemic stability measures
Protecting corporate deposits and payment operations

round_structure: Crisis briefing: Failed institution exposure assessment
Counterparty risk analysis: Direct and indirect exposures
Liquidity stress test: Funding needs under scenarios
Collateral and margin review: Call risk assessment
Regulatory coordination: Central bank and supervisor engagement
Market confidence measures: Communication strategy
Contingency funding: Emergency liquidity options
Portfolio defensive moves: Risk reduction actions
Recovery planning: Post-crisis positioning strategy

required: Direct counterparty exposure to failed institution
Interbank lending and repo positions
Liquidity coverage and net stable funding ratios
Collateral and margin arrangements
optional: Stress testing results for credit shocks

environment_config.decision_mechanism: Crisis management committee with board notification
environment_config.coalition_dynamics: Prudent risk reduction vs. market confidence preservation

scenarios.name: Fintech Disruption in Banking
scenarios.description: A digital challenger bank enters traditional markets with superior UX, lower fees, and innovative services. Incumbents must decide on competitive response, partnership, or acquisition strategies.

customization.context: Defending market share against digital challenger
Evaluating digital transformation response options
Modeling acquisition vs. organic investment economics
Protecting customer base and branch network
Aggressive fintech disruptor seeking market share
Evaluating fintech investment and bank partnership opportunities

round_structure: Competitive threat assessment: Challenger capabilities and positioning
Customer segment analysis: Vulnerability by product and demographic
Response option evaluation: Build, buy, partner, or ignore
Digital capabilities gap analysis: Technology and UX assessment
Financial modeling: Investment requirements and returns
M&A target evaluation: Acquisition feasibility and pricing
Partnership structure options: Collaboration models
Implementation planning: Timeline and resource allocation
Strategic commitment: Definitive response decision

required: Challenger bank product and pricing details
Customer segment profitability and churn risk analysis
Current digital platform capabilities assessment
M&A comparable transactions and valuations

environment_config.decision_mechanism: Board approval for major M&A or capital commitments
environment_config.escalation_trigger: Accelerated customer loss triggers emergency review
environment_config.coalition_dynamics: Digital transformation advocates vs. traditional banking defenders

scenarios.name: ESG Regulatory Tightening
scenarios.description: New ESG disclosure requirements and sustainable finance regulations impact investment strategies, lending criteria, and capital allocation. Financial institutions must adapt frameworks and products.

customization.context: Leading asset manager through ESG regulatory transformation
Developing ESG integration and reporting frameworks
Creating green bond and ESG product offerings
Ensuring ESG disclosure compliance and avoiding greenwashing
Enforcing new sustainable finance disclosure requirements
Demanding robust ESG integration and transparency

round_structure: Regulatory briefing: New ESG disclosure requirements
Portfolio ESG assessment: Current exposure and gaps
Taxonomy alignment: Eligible activities classification
Data and reporting infrastructure: Systems and processes
Product strategy: Green finance and ESG fund development
Stewardship approach: Engagement and voting policies
Transition finance framework: Supporting brown-to-green
Compliance roadmap: Implementation timeline and milestones

required: Full text of new ESG disclosure regulations
Current portfolio ESG data and coverage
EU Taxonomy or equivalent classification framework
Peer ESG integration and product offerings

environment_config.decision_mechanism: Executive committee with sustainability board oversight
environment_config.escalation_trigger: Regulatory non-compliance findings trigger immediate action
environment_config.coalition_dynamics: Traditional investment managers vs. ESG integration advocates

scenarios.name: Digital Currency Impact
scenarios.description: Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) introduction disrupts payment systems, deposit funding, and transaction banking. Financial institutions must adapt business models and infrastructure.

customization.context: Leading bank through CBDC-induced payment disruption
Managing payment infrastructure transition
Integrating CBDC infrastructure and APIs
Assessing deposit disintermediation risks
Overseeing CBDC rollout and bank integration
Leveraging CBDC for payment innovation

round_structure: CBDC architecture briefing: Technical and operational requirements
Payment revenue impact: Fee compression analysis
Deposit funding risk: Disintermediation scenarios
Technology integration: Infrastructure and API requirements
New service opportunities: CBDC-enabled products
Competitive positioning: First-mover vs. fast-follower
Customer experience design: Wallet and interface strategy
Implementation roadmap: Phased rollout plan

required: CBDC technical specifications and API documentation
Current payment revenue breakdown by product
Deposit base composition and stickiness analysis
Technology infrastructure readiness assessment
optional: CBDC pilot program results from other jurisdictions

environment_config.decision_mechanism: Executive committee with technology steering committee
environment_config.escalation_trigger: Major payment volume loss triggers emergency review
environment_config.coalition_dynamics: Traditional payments business vs. digital innovation advocates

scenarios.name: Cybersecurity Breach Response
scenarios.description: A major data breach at a financial institution exposes customer data and threatens operational integrity. The bank must execute incident response, manage regulatory notifications, and preserve customer trust.

customization.context: Leading breach response and reputation recovery
Containing breach and securing systems
Managing regulatory notifications and legal exposure
Managing external and internal communications
Managing customer inquiries and retention
Overseeing breach response and enforcement

round_structure: Breach assessment: Scope and severity determination
Containment actions: Immediate security measures
Regulatory notification: Compliance with disclosure requirements
Customer communication: Transparent and timely disclosure
Forensic investigation: Root cause and attribution analysis
Remediation planning: System hardening and control improvements
Customer protection: Credit monitoring and compensation
Reputation recovery: Trust rebuilding strategy

required: Breach forensics and affected data inventory
Regulatory notification requirements and timelines
Customer communication templates and channels
Incident response plan and team contacts

environment_config.escalation_trigger: System compromise or data exfiltration triggers emergency protocols
environment_config.coalition_dynamics: Transparent disclosure vs. legal risk minimization tensions

scenarios.name: Cross-Border Regulatory Divergence
scenarios.description: Conflicting regulations across jurisdictions create compliance complexity for global financial institutions. Banks must navigate divergent capital, liquidity, and conduct requirements.

customization.context: Managing global operations under regulatory fragmentation
Harmonizing risk frameworks across jurisdictions
Coordinating multi-jurisdictional compliance
Navigating US-specific regulatory requirements
Managing EU regulatory complexity
Coordinating cross-border supervisory approach

round_structure: Regulatory mapping: Divergence analysis by jurisdiction
Capital and liquidity requirements: Basel implementation gaps
Conduct and consumer protection: Conflicting standards
Data and privacy: Cross-border transfer restrictions
Structural options: Subsidiary vs. branch models
Compliance architecture: Global vs. local frameworks
Regulatory engagement: Coordination and advocacy
Strategic positioning: Market prioritization decisions

required: Regulatory divergence matrix by jurisdiction
Current legal entity structure and capital allocation
Basel implementation differences across regions
Data localization and privacy requirements

environment_config.decision_mechanism: Global executive committee with regional representation
environment_config.escalation_trigger: Regulatory enforcement action triggers immediate response
environment_config.coalition_dynamics: Global efficiency vs. local regulatory autonomy tensions
